title: [FIRRTL][LowerXMR] Lower internal path into a module
body:
This commit adds support to generate XMR with opaque internal paths into a module.
A combination of `verbatim.expr` and `ref.send` is used to encode an internal path into a module. `LowerXMR` must generate the correct hierarchical path for such internal paths into a module.
If an internal path (for example `random.path.inside`) exists inside a module `Foo`, the hierarchical path must end at the op that instantiates `Foo`, and the `random.path.inside` must be appended to it. This avoids any temporary `NodeOp` or wires inside the module `Foo`.
This replaces the requirement for a new op as discussed in https://github.com/llvm/circt/pull/4196
